The '''Both system''', also known as the '''Bothawui system''', or as the '''Bothan system''', was a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] located within the [[Bothan Space/Legends|Bothan sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="EAOC" /> 

It was also within the region known as the [[The Slice/Legends|Slice]].<ref name="TEA" /> It was situated at the junction of four major [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]]:<ref name="guide" /> the [[Bothan Run]], the [[Kaaga Run]], the [[Reena Trade Route]], and the [[Manda Merchant Route]].<ref name="TEA" /> 
==History==
Containing a number of [[Planet/Legends|planets]], including [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] and [[Golm]],<ref name="TEA" /> the unstable third planet broke, leaving in its place an [[asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]. The cataclysm affected the axial plain of Bothawui (which became the third planet).<ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

[[Pre-Republic era|Pre-Republic]] explorers first discovered the farthest planets of the system, [[Hoppawui]] and [[Jentawui]].<ref name="guide" />

[[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]] was in charge of [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] operations there.

[[File:Battle_of_Bothawui.jpg|left|thumb|[[Raid on the Suprosa]]]]
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], in his personal [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], accompanied by [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] in his [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]], the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']], and twelve [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighters]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by [[Koth Melan]] and ten [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] sympathetic to the Rebellion and Alliance pilot [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]], led an [[Raid on the Suprosa|attack to intercept the freighter]] as it exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] on the fringes of the Both system. When the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] intercepted the freighter, named the ''[[Suprosa]]'', and demanded its surrender, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] unveiled its heavy armaments and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. Before Skywalker and Azzameen could disable the ship, the freighter had destroyed six of the Y-wings. After the computer was secured, it was taken to the Bothan colony world of [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]] where a special team would attempt to decrypt it and it was revealed that the [[Death Star II plans|plans]] were the construction details to a new, more powerful [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="SotE">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref><ref>[[Shadows of the Empire 4|''Shadows of the Empire'' 4]]</ref>

After a series of conflicts with the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] immediately thereafter, the plans were finally delivered to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. It would turn out that the Emperor had intentionally allowed the plans to fall into the hands of the Alliance in order to lead them into a trap at the Death Star II construction site over the moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], where he would finally attempt to destroy them once and for all.<ref>{{film|VI}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The databank in the game ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' mistakenly claimed that the Both system also contains the planet [[Krant]].
